h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would eventually stop seeing the decimal as work and accept it as a mere label position.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner repeats the same labeling task without adopting a new device or criterion; the learning problem persists.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor never explicitly changes route; all tasks reinforce the same principle of fixed label positions.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No learner pivot, so no contingent adaptation.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner repeats the same labeling task without adopting a new device or criterion; the learning problem persists.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No learner pivot, so no contingent adaptation.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor never explicitly changes route; all tasks reinforce the same principle of fixed label positions.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor never explicitly changes route; all tasks reinforce the same principle of fixed label positions.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner might initially name forces by arrow direction, but the tutor guides them to use the 'force on cart from' schema.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: I was going to call it the right arrow, but the blank is hand: force on cart from hand.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner enacts the new labeling device by identifying the source of force rather than arrow direction, completing the task correctly.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner resistance or breakdown; the tutor's initial framing suffices.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn, so no contingent adaptation.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: I was going to call it the right arrow, but the blank is hand: force on cart from hand.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner enacts the new labeling device by identifying the source of force rather than arrow direction, completing the task correctly.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn, so no contingent adaptation.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner resistance or breakdown; the tutor's initial framing suffices.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner resistance or breakdown; the tutor's initial framing suffices.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner progressively fills in the time signature and places bar lines as instructed.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner performs stated tasks without independently introducing a new device, criterion, or task.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure for a reversal; the tutoring proceeds smoothly without breakdown.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists, so no recognition-contingent adaptation.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner performs stated tasks without independently introducing a new device, criterion, or task.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn exists, so no recognition-contingent adaptation.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner turn creates pressure for a reversal; the tutoring proceeds smoothly without breakdown.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner turn creates pressure for a reversal; the tutoring proceeds smoothly without breakdown.
